My case was a complicated one. I’m a foreigner who received a conditional green card through marriage, but I went through a divorce before obtaining my permanent green card. After applying for Removal of Conditions, I received a Request for Evidence from USCIS and decided to seek help.

Carmen was incredibly professional and attentive. She paid close attention to every detail of my intricate case, and her guidance was always precise and tailored to the evidence I had, especially important since my ex-spouse would not respond or cooperate in any way. Even with that added difficulty, she helped me structure everything correctly.

Recently, I received my approval notice from USCIS. After years of frustration, back-and-forth, anxiety, sleepless nights, and thousands of dollars spent throughout the entire process, the conditions were finally removed. My green card is now fully mine and no longer tied to my ex-spouse.

It’s also worth mentioning that these cases require a lot of personal effort, hundreds of hours gathering documents, organizing evidence, and being extremely meticulous. Carmen does her part exceptionally well, but you also need to do yours.

I’m extremely grateful for her help and would 100% recommend her to anyone seeking immigration advice.

What a nice little law office. It’s pretty low-key, with a decent location. I was able to park right out front of the office on Plumas Street.

I had an appointment to go in and sign some papers to finalize a difficult legal situation. It’s usually not pleasant when you have to visit a attorney, at least until you can perhaps resolve a situation and move onto something better.

Anyway, I spoke to the office manager, Tony, and he made me feel very comfortable in the office, even where I was on the opposing side of a process. Even so, he was very polite and professional, even though it was a stressful situation.

This can often be common when you have to go to the lawyer’s office. The legal situation in Washoe County leaves a lot to be desired. People who are un represented often lose their rights because of their inability to pay for representation by professional lawyers.

The courts in Washoe County, unfortunately, do not show much mercy for those who are unable to afford full-time representation from attorneys. The pro bono, lawyers, who I applaud them for their sacrifice, can only offer limited advice and support.

The biggest problem with the pro bono attorneys is that they are so overworked and understaffed they cannot keep up with the demand for the clients that are in the worst situations. The law is biased towards the rich. It’s a fact, and don’t let anyone try to convince me otherwise. I’ve been there, done that.

Nevertheless, in spite of my soapbox speech, I sat down in this office to sign papers, and when I discovered errors, I brought them up to the office manager, Tony. He really knows his stuff, and he took the time to look things over and make phone calls to correct the situation.

Nothing in this world is perfect! I’m not criticizing or complaining because those errors were fixed by the time the paperwork was filed. Even though I was in opposition to this attorney‘s client, I gave them a five star rating because of their professionalism and their great office decorations! OK, just kidding, but the place did look nice.

Good job you guys, keep up the good work, and don’t forget the little guy, everyone deserves equal representation under the law, yeah, right!

Jessica has represented me for 5 years in a highly contentious litigation with my the father of two of my children, who is a litigator himself. I want to preface my review by first sharing that I 100% remember how absolutely daunting and stressful it was to have to choose a stranger to help me defend my life, and the only thing I truly cared about- my children. I had worked with two other attorneys before finding Jessica, and my trust and hope broke down with each of them. Jessica, over the course of half a decade, has proven herself to be not only exhaustively accurate and careful, but realistic, empathetic, fair, invested, humane, kind-hearted, supportive, beyond competent in litigating and handling a multitude of different types of people and cases, reliable, trustworthy, communicative, and patient. To find all of these qualities subsumed into a person, much less an attorney, much less a family law attorney, is a rarity that I cannot even begin to articulate. My case involves two children, is being litigated in two states simultaneously, and their father is a relentless, vexatious litigant. The legal history and and facts of the case are as complicated as they could possibly be. She has handled every motion, hearing, trial, appeal, conference, and communication without a hint of weakness, frustration, or exhaustion. It’s typically common knowledge that no money equals no justice, but I had next to nothing and the father of my kids earned millions of dollars per year at huge law firms and had every resource imaginable- she did not allow me to drown and did not allow his conduct to dissuade her. She went out of her way to ensure that she did not ever incur unnecessary fees. Many attorneys market themselves as “winners” in efforts to obtain business- that descriptor is neither trustworthy nor possible, nor is it accurate, because no attorney has complete control over the law or what a judge will exercise discretion over. What does matter is an attorney’s understanding of the law and process, ability to be precise, reliable, and prepared, ability to both litigate and encourage settlement, and ability to de-escalate needlessly contentious and expensive issues with both opposing counsel and opposing pro-se/pro-per. Jessica has mastered all of these skills to perfection and after having been represented by multiple attorneys in two different states through a series of conferences, depositions, trials, hearings, and settlements, I can say that she is a very rare find. There may possibly be less expensive attorneys in the area, but they will undoubtedly bill substantially more hours for work that she can do efficiently and skillfully. You will end up paying more if you shop for the cheapest per-hour representation.
In conclusion, I absolutely, undoubtedly, passionately recommend her.
Jessica Anderson is very caring and a knowledgeable family law attorney.  We highly recommend her.
Fast friendly and knowledgeable

reviews.ownerResponse:
Under Nevada law, a personal injury claim for emotional distress requires medical documentation from a qualified healthcare provider who can diagnose and relate those injuries to the accident. As explained over the phone, without seeking medical treatment, we are unable to ethically or legally pursue such a claim on a client’s behalf. Given you do not wish to seek medical attention, we were unable to help you.

We did our best to provide alternative resources, which included the Nevada Lawyer Referral Service, should your circumstances change. Our goal is always to be transparent, honest, and respectful while following the law and our professional obligations.

We wish you the best moving forward. ~Ben Bingham, Esq.

reviews.text:
On April 21, 2025, my puppies and I were involved in a terrifying, life-altering car accident. An unlicensed, unregistered driver ran a red light and hit us with such force that we spun into oncoming traffic, collided with another vehicle, and were thrown backwards over the median into a third car. Every airbag deployed, my truck was totaled, and we were left shaken and injured — but grateful to be alive.

From the ER, my sister insisted I contact an attorney immediately. Benson & Bingham in Reno was open, and from the very first moment Heidi answered the phone, the service was truly exceptional. She scheduled me an appointment for the next day with my attorney, Todd Alexander, who immediately took action and connected me with the care I desperately needed through Dr. Vondra.  Todd immediately authorized all care from that point on, such as an MRI and a nerve test.  There was delay in getting any care I needed!

I was also assigned a care manager, Nellie Adamie, and she has been nothing short of incredible. Nellie has taken my calls when I was overwhelmed and breaking down, offering compassion, reassurance, and guidance every single time. She has checked in on me consistently, kept me updated, and made me feel supported throughout this entire process.

My vehicle may be gone, and the accident was caused by someone who never should have been behind the wheel, but thanks to Benson & Bingham, I have never felt alone in navigating the aftermath.

If you ever find yourself in a similar situation, do not hesitate to contact Benson & Bingham. I have complete confidence in their team — they treat you with genuine care and ensure you receive exactly what you need, every step of the way. They truly go above and beyond.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We understand how frustrating this is for you, but Nevada requires that damages be actual and identifiable and not speculative. Unfortunately, "almost" being injured or what "could have" occurred is not a basis for a claim. A near-miss, standing alone, does not meet the legal threshold for recovery any more than a driver who runs a red light cannot be held liable unless there is actual contact or injury.

Even in cases where the law bars us from pursuing a claim, we make every effort to candidly advise our clients of the strengths and weaknesses in their cases. We would be happy to discuss this matter further with you directly if you wish to discuss it further or if you believe there is additional information we should consider. ~Ben Bingham, Esq.

My case is a bit of a long shot, due to my young age and the ways my disabilities are usually treated, but we're just gonna keep on trucking and take it as far as we can. We recently updated our estate documents with Laury Macauley, Esq.  We have had trusts for over four decades and have worked with a number of attorneys over the years.  After moving to Reno Nevada, a community property state, we needed an update.  We contacted a number of estate attorneys.  Almost all had 60+ page boiler plate trust templates into which they inserted relevant individualized information but most of these bloated documents had information that does not and never will be relevant to us.  Such large trusts are cumbersome and inefficient since during settlement, parties must read everything to be sure something important is not missed.  We wanted a more personalized straightforward trust which eliminated all the bloat, unnecessary legal jargon, generation skipping wording, minor dependents, dependent trusts, etc. etc.

Laury agreed to work with us to accomplish what we wanted…estate documents relevant to our situation written in a straightforward manner.  In the end our very comprehensive trust is 22 pages long.  We found her to be bright, helpful, knowledgeable and ethical.  She was among a very few lawyers willing to help us create what WE wanted rather than something off the shelf.  This took extra time so the process did extend over 2 months but we are extremely pleased.  We would definitely recommend Ms. Macauley without reservation to others.

Initially, I scheduled a consultation with Melissa at Surratt in January to draft a prenup agreement. This consultation got pushed several times and did not happen until late February, and in that consultation I was told that it was a simple agreement that would only take one to two months to draft and be executed by both parties. Shortly after the consultation, I signed a retainer agreement to start drafting the agreement. It took 3 weeks for anyone at Surratt to reach out to me to confirm they even got the retainer payment. We then did not get any kind of traction on a draft for several more weeks. At each turnaround, it took at minimum 2 weeks for Surratt to respond to emails with feedback on the document, and they often would not respond unless prompted with a follow up email. Getting this ‘simple’ agreement drafted became a second job for me with the amount of communication I had to do with Surratt. After multiple back and forths, a final agreement was reached and signing date was set. When I went into the office to sign the agreement, I was told their internet had been down all day, they had not printed the agreement and could not print it, their notary was out of the office, and I would need to schedule a different time to come in for a signing appointment. Finally 5 months after the consultation there is an executed agreement, but it never should have been that much of a challenge. I can only imagine how hard it is to work with Surratt for anyone with a ‘difficult’ agreement to draft and would strongly recommend towards finding a different family law practice in town.

Reply to Review Update 05/28/2024:
Sarah:
I am so sorry that you feel that your mother and aunt are not receiving a response from our law firm. We had not heard anything from either of them for over a year. Then suddenly a copy of the final income tax return, the last item the court requires to submit a final report, was dropped off at our office just about a month ago. With that last item, we were able to place the completion of the final report in our work queue. Then your aunt dropped into our office to meet me without a scheduled appointment. Unfortunately, I was not available to meet with her. (We always recommend making appointments in advance as I am often in client meetings.) However, Lisa confirmed with her that the final report was working through our queue. Your aunt followed up with a telephone call on May 28 inquiring about the status of the final report. Our receptionist, called her back on May 29 to notify her that the final report should be done shortly. It appears that our office has been very responsive. Our office has reached out to your aunt to confirm the status of the filing of the final report with the court.

Bryce Rader, Esq.
Anderson, Dorn & Rader, Ltd..
__________________________________________________________________
Reply to 2022 Review:

Hello Sarah,
Thank you for your review, all feedback is important to us, and we’re sorry to hear your family had such a frustrating experience. As you can see from our other reviews, your situation is the exception and far from our usual standards.

You indicated that the reasons for your dissatisfaction were that your mother and aunt had to do a lot of the footwork, lost documents, and prolonged timing. We can prepare and file the necessary legal documents for a probate matter and answer questions. However, we are not authorized to transact the necessary administration of a decedent's estate. Only the Court appointed representative has legal authority. It would be very rare for our office to lose a document since all documents submitted to the Court must be digital (at least in Washoe County). Regarding the timing of Court dates, a probate process is determined by our State statute and the availability of the Court's calendar. Neither of which is in our control. We highly encourage your mother and aunt to contact our office if they have any questions regarding the probate process and their final steps.

Lastly, you suggest that our law firm may be biased towards assisting with certain types of probate matters. We’re sorry you felt this way after working with our office. As many of our clients can attest, we will assist with the probate process from the very modest estate up to the most sophisticated estate.

Please call us at (775) 823-9455 or email us at info@wealth-counselors.com to discuss what occurred and how we might make it up to you.

reviews.text: April is an exceptional life care planner, combining expertise with unwavering dedication. Even when faced with challenging opposition, she remains professional and focused on her clients' best interests. Highly recommend for her integrity and skill.

“The field of Legal Nurse Consultants and Life Care Planners is growing, and with good reason.  During my 30+ years in practice as an attorney handling medical malpractice and personal injury cases of all types, I have utilized the services of Legal Nurse Consultants and Life Care Planners on numerous occasions.  Working with Legal Nurse Consultants and Life Care Planners allows me to not only save money for my clients, but also obtain better results due to the expertise that nurses bring to the table.  Legal Nurse Consultants and Life Care Planners often serve in the important role of being an expert witness.  But perhaps even more important is their role as consultants in helping the entire legal team identify and understand critical medical issues.  Certification as a Legal Nurse Consultant and/or Life Care Planner not only adds a credential that you can include on your CV, thereby bolstering your credibility as a witness, but earning certification will help you understand the legal process better and thereby increase your value to any legal team.  A Legal Nurse Consultant and/or Life Care Planner who understands the legal process can be an invaluable member of any legal team. Working as a Legal Nurse Consultant also allows – in fact encourages – you to continue your education and interest in the medical specialty areas of your choice.  For example, we recently reached out to April Stallings about a case specifically because of her experience and knowledge in traumatic brain injuries as well as her experience as a Legal Nurse Consultant and Life Care Planner.  I would encourage anyone who is interested in the field of Legal Nurse Consulting and/or Life Care Planning to reach out to April and her team to explore the benefits of the profession and earning certification from a superbly qualified expert in the profession through the learning platform that April has developed.”

April is a very responsive and extremely knowledgeable life care planner.  I found working with her to be easy and she was able to spot issues early.  She is also fair and circumspect in what she included in our life care plan, adding to her (and our) credibility.  We will use her again.
